# TORCH — Economy QA Report

Ran the deterministic core renderless: seeds [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] × 4000 ticks each.

**Verdict: LIVABLE — balanced overall, with concerns to watch.** (7 ✓ · 3 ⚠ · 0 ✗)

✓ **Determinism**
   Same seed ⇒ identical end state (total credits 54080602).

⚠ **Solvency**
   Lowest any power's credits ever fell: -75937 (The Free Navy, seed 1). A power ran a deficit funding its infrastructure — bounded, not bankrupt.

✓ **National self-funding**
   Lowest a nation's end credits fell across seeds: 816695 (Outer Planets Alliance, seed 0). Population income keeps the nations self-funding (fleets + refuelling + builds).

✓ **Money supply**
   Total credits grew to ~737% of the start over 4000 ticks (avg of 8 seeds). Bounded — the production-backed money supply grows steadily, not runaway.

✓ **Market liveness**
   Every good's price varies and never pins to its floor/ceiling — a living market.

⚠ **Trade spreads (arbitrage)**
   Avg inter-market price spread per good: Ice ~10 · Ore ~49 · Silicon ~19 · Gold ~61 · Silver ~55 · Platinum ~74 · Fusion Fuel ~93 · Alloys ~103 · Silicon Wafers ~126 · Bullion ~230 · Electronics ~396 · Machine Parts ~232 · Ship Components ~875 · Food ~55. Some spreads stay wide — arbitrage isn't fully closing them (too few traders?).

⚠ **Food security**
   Peak 5/19 settlements out of food (26%). Some settlements occasionally run dry — logistics is tight.

✓ **Hauler utilization**
   ~2/88 haulers idle on an average tick (2%). Haulers are busy — the trade network has work for them.

✓ **Industry throughput**
   ~8/17 facilities had input to produce on an average tick (47%). Facilities are kept fed — the production chain flows.

✓ **Reservation integrity**
   Market reservations never oversubscribed stock or drove availability negative.
